preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the alveolar nasal [n] is documented as a form of nasal assimilation in consonant clusters, particularly where the sibilant adopts the nasal feature of an adjacent alveolar segment.
phoneticEffects: Nasal Assimilation, Place Alignment, Consonantalization Shift

examples.note: Historical shifts where voiced sibilants before nasals underwent total assimilation or triggered vowel lengthening upon loss.
Occasional assimilation in rapid or colloquial speech where the dental/alveolar fricative merges into the following nasal segment.

sources: Phonological research on Greek and Arabic nasal-sibilant clusters.
